config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap dilindungi sebagai batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kondisi sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil yang justru perlu dilindungi. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come first over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, our strongest protections exist precisely because majorities and governments can abuse power when they are told the collective goal justifies everything, and that makes minority rights and civil liberties nonnegotiable. At the same time, individual rights are not a license to harm others, so public safety and the common good do matter, but they need strict limits and due process. If the balance is reversed too far, "society" becomes a convenient excuse for censorship, discrimination, or overreach.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya tidak adil, terutama terhadap kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru harus menjadi batas agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena atas nama kebaikan bersama. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, namun cara mencapainya harus tetap menghormati hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, that framing is dangerous because it invites governments and majorities to override basic liberties whenever they claim a collective benefit, and history shows that claim gets abused fast. I do think society can impose limits for public safety, fraud prevention, and protecting other people's rights, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and accountable. If you make "society" the default priority, individual rights stop being real rights and become permissions granted by whoever has power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat istilah “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk menekan kelompok kecil, padahal kelompok kecil itu sering paling rentan dan paling butuh perlindungan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk menghapus hak dasar, karena kalau batas itu hilang, yang kuat akan selalu menang atas yang lemah. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, dengan hak individu sebagai pagar utama dan kepentingan sosial dibatasi oleh keadilan serta akuntabilitas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, that idea is too easy to weaponize, because once officials or majorities get to define "the common good" however they want, basic liberties can be stripped away from unpopular people with very little accountability. I do think society can and should set limits for real harms like violence, fraud, and public safety, but those limits have to be narrow and justified, not a blank check. To me, rights are not a reward for good citizenship; they are the guardrails that keep society from becoming oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
